operation: First, create a workplane on the XY plane, draw a circle with 12mm radius, extrude it 8mm to form the base cylinder. Next, establish a new XY workplane offset 8mm along the Z axis, draw a regular 5-sided polygon with 10mm radius, extrude it 6mm, and union this pentagonal prism to the top of the base cylinder. Finally, select the topmost Z-facing face of the combined part, create a workplane on this face, and cut a coaxial counterbore hole: the through hole has a 5mm diameter, the counterbore has an 8mm diameter and 4mm depth, with total hole depth of 10mm extending downward from the top face.
geometry: The model is a coaxial stacked solid composed of a lower right circular cylinder and a smaller regular pentagonal prism centered on the top face of the cylinder. A coaxial stepped counterbore hole runs vertically through the entire pentagonal prism and partially into the cylindrical base, with a wider recessed section at the top of the hole.
function: This is a tamper-resistant mounting plug or knob base, designed to be secured with a standard fastener via its integrated counterbore hole, while its pentagonal upper head requires a specialized matching tool for installation and removal to prevent unauthorized adjustment.
